description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:latest image with a docker:dind service, defines the stages test, build, and deploy, and sets the HOST and IMAGE variables as given. In the test stage add a test:flake8 job using python:3 that runs `pip install flake8` then `flake8`, and a test:unit-tests job using python:3 that runs `pip install -r requirements.txt` followed by `pytest nebula/ --cov=nebula`. In the build stage add a build:production job (docker:latest) that runs only on the master branch, executes `docker login -u "$CI_REGISTRY_USER" -p "$CI_REGISTRY_PASSWORD" $CI_REGISTRY`, moves the production settings file with `mv nebula/nebula/settings_production.py nebula/nebula/settings_injected.py`, builds the image with `docker build -f Dockerfile -t $IMAGE .`, and pushes it with `docker push $IMAGE`. In the deploy stage add a deploy-production-hard job (debian:latest, manual, only on master) that sources SSH helpers via `source bin/allow_ssh.sh` and `source bin/scp_secrets.sh $HOST`, copies the compose file with `scp docker-compose.yml $HOST:~/docker-compose.yml`, and runs `ssh $HOST "sudo docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml NEBULA --with-registry-auth"`. Also add a deploy:production job (debian:latest, only on master) that performs the same SSH setup and copy steps and then runs `ssh $HOST "sudo docker service update --with-registry-auth --image $IMAGE NEBULA_nebula"`.
